title: TIE fighter kill = 1,000 points
claim: A regular TIE kill scores 1,000. ROM table TSCA1D = .BYTE 00,10,00; ours TIE_SCORE = 1000. Match. This also confirms the prior sw2-6 claim 'TIE worth 1,000 not 100' — RIGHT, and already ported.
reasoning: BCD decode: each score row is three BCD byte-pairs (MSD pair first) forming a 6-digit decimal that ADUSCR (WSGAS:484-503) adds to the BCD score S.SC21/43/65/87 with DAA — no x10 shift, so the added value IS the literal 6-digit number, resolving the BCD-trap here. TSCA1D 00,10,00 -> '00'|'10'|'00' = 001000 = 1,000. SCRTIES:: (WSGAS:444, loads TSCA1D) truncates to the 6-char symbol SCRTIE, which XPSA calls (WSCPU:443 'JSR SCRTIE ;ADD SCORE FOR KILLING TIE FIGHTER') on the killing hit. Ours awards TIE_SCORE=1000 per bolt-kill (sim.ts:268).

title: Darth Vader's TIE = 2,000 points (value matches; not yet awarded)
claim: ROM scores Darth at 2,000 PER HIT (TSCA2D = .BYTE 00,20,00 -> 002000). Ours VADER_SCORE = 2000. Value matches. NOTE: the sim has no Vader enemy, so this constant is defined but never awarded — all enemies use TIE_SCORE=1000.
reasoning: TSCA2D 00,20,00 = 002000 = 2,000. SCRDARTH (WSGAS:449) is called from WSCPU:369 ('JSR SCRDARTH ;ADD SCORE FOR HITTING DARTH') inside the damage path that also does 'LDA #05 / STA A$HTA(X) ;KEEP DARTH ALIVE' — so 2,000 is awarded on EACH hit that damages Darth (he is refreshed to 5 hits-available and eventually flees, 'CHASED DARTH OFF'), not once per kill. Our VADER_SCORE constant equals the ROM value but no code path awards it (Enemy.kind is 'tie' only; Vader exists only as a render model), so per-hit Darth scoring is presently absent behavior with a correct constant staged for it.

title: Shot-down fireball = 33 points
claim: Shooting an enemy fireball out of the air scores 33. ROM TSCSHT = .BYTE 00,00,33 -> 000033 = 33; ours FIREBALL_SCORE = 33. Match. Confirms the sw2-6 value.
reasoning: TSCSHT 00,00,33 = 000033 = 33 (BCD, cheapest kill on the board). SCRSHT (WSGAS:469) is called from WSGUNS:1194 ('JSR SCRSHT ;SCORE OUR SHOT'). Ours adds FIREBALL_SCORE=33 when a player bolt downs a fireball (sim.ts:296).

title: Tower top = 200 points
claim: Destroying a surface tower top scores 200. ROM TSCTWR = .BYTE 00,02,00 -> 000200 = 200; ours TURRET_SCORE = 200. Match.
reasoning: TSCTWR:: 00,02,00 = 000200 = 200 (BCD). SCRTWR (WSGAS:343) is called from WSGRND:1174 ('JSR SCRTWR ;SCORE FOR HITTING A TOWER BUNKER'); it also folds an incremental TWRMULT bonus and, when the last tower drops, gives the all-towers bonus (see S-009). Ours scores every surface object at TURRET_SCORE=200 (sim.ts:522).

title: Laser bunker = 200 points
claim: Destroying a laser bunker scores 200 — same as a tower. ROM TSCBNK = .BYTE 00,02,00 -> 000200 = 200; ours applies TURRET_SCORE=200 to all surface kinds including bunkers. Match.
reasoning: TSCBNK 00,02,00 = 000200 = 200 (BCD), equal to the tower value. SCRBNK (WSGAS:459) is called from WSGRND:1169 ('JSR SCRBNK ;SCORE FOR HITTING A BUNKER'). Ours does not distinguish score by kind — 'score += TURRET_SCORE' runs for every surface object (sim.ts:522); the kind check on the next line only excludes bunkers from the tower QUOTA, not from the points, matching the ROM (200 either way).

title: Trench gun turret = 100 points
claim: A trench gun turret scores 100. ROM TSCTRT = .BYTE 00,01,00 -> 000100 = 100; ours TRENCH_TURRET_SCORE = 100. Match.
reasoning: TSCTRT 00,01,00 = 000100 = 100 (BCD). SCRTRT (WSGAS:464) is called from WSLAZR:697 ('JSR SCRTRT ;KILLED A TURRET'). Ours awards TRENCH_TURRET_SCORE=100 when a bolt kills a trench turret obstacle (sim.ts:669).

title: Decorative trench panel / wall square = 50 points
claim: A decorative trench wall panel scores 50. ROM TSCPNL = .BYTE 00,00,50 -> 000050 = 50; ours TRENCH_SQUARE_SCORE = 50. Match.
reasoning: TSCPNL 00,00,50 = 000050 = 50 (BCD). SCRPNL (WSGAS:454) is called from WSLAZR:688 ('JSR SCRPNL ;SCORE FOR A PANEL'). Ours awards TRENCH_SQUARE_SCORE=50 for a non-turret trench obstacle (sim.ts:669).

title: Exhaust port hit = 25,000 points
claim: Killing the Death Star (exhaust port hit) scores 25,000. ROM TSCPRT = .BYTE 02,50,00 -> 025000 = 25,000; ours TRENCH_BONUS = 25000. Match. Confirms the prior sw2-6 claim 'exhaust port 25,000 not 1,000' — RIGHT, and already ported.
reasoning: TSCPRT 02,50,00 = '02'|'50'|'00' = 025000 = 25,000 (BCD; the BCD trap makes this read as 25,000, not 2,500 or 250,000). SCRPORT (WSGAS:474) is called from WSMAIN:1960 ('JSR SCRPORT ;SCORE THE EXHAUST PORT'), gated on PT.LIV (fired at the port). Ours adds TRENCH_BONUS=25000 on a port kill (sim.ts:751, 'bonus = TRENCH_BONUS + ...'). Note ROM keeps the exhaust-port score (25,000) SEPARATE from the level-scaled 'use the Force' bonus (see S-012); ours composes both at the same site.

title: All-towers-destroyed bonus = 50,000 points
claim: Eliminating every tower top / bunker in a wave scores a 50,000 bonus. ROM TSCATP = .BYTE 05,00,00 -> 050000 = 50,000; ours SURFACE_CLEAR_BONUS = 50000. Match. Confirms the prior sw2-6 claim 'the 50,000 all-towers bonus' — it exists in the ROM and is now ported (the prior 'missing' state is remediated).
reasoning: TSCATP 05,00,00 = 050000 = 50,000 (BCD). The bonus fires inside SCRTWR (WSGAS:361-370): after decrementing GD.TWL ('# of towers left') via decimal-adjust, 'IFEQ ;?DONE ALL TOWERS? / LDU #TSCATP / JSR ADUSCR' awards it and sets Q.ATP. Ours banks SURFACE_CLEAR_BONUS=50000 once, on the surface->trench drop, when allTowersKilled() (sim.ts:947).

source.verbatim: TSCATP:	.BYTE 05,00,00		;ELIMINATED ALL TOWER TOPS AND/OR BUNKERS

title: Starting shields = 6 (default operator option)
claim: The player starts with 6 shield units at the default option. ROM: 'ADDA #06 ;GIVES 6 TO 9 UNITS OF SHIELD STRENGTH' (S.GAS init) with a 0-3 operator-option add; default (no option) = 6. Ours STARTING_LIVES = 6. Match at the default.
reasoning: WSMAIN:1293-1297 sets S.GAS = 6 + (OPTS1+1 & 03), i.e. 6 to 9, per the operator GAS option; the same clamp bounds ADCGAS refills (WSGAS:48-53). A browser clone has no operator DIPs, so the default 6 is the faithful value and ours matches. LIMITATION: ours has no way to select the 7/8/9 option settings (no operator panel) — recorded, not audited.

source.verbatim: ADDA #06		;GIVES 6 TO 9 UNITS OF SHIELD STRENGTH

title: Each cockpit hit costs exactly 1 shield unit
claim: A hit subtracts one shield unit. ROM DO1GAS: 'DEC S.GAS ;THEN DECREASE OUR FUEL'. Ours: each cockpit collision adds 1 to damage, and lives -= damage. The per-hit cost of 1 matches.
reasoning: In DO1GAS (WSGAS:63-104) a registered shield hit (GS.GLW>0) does a single DEC S.GAS (WSGAS:76); if that underflows it is pinned to -1 ('SET TO DEAD'). Ours increments damage by 1 per colliding TIE/fireball (sim.ts:310,318) then applies lives = max(0, state.lives - damage) (sim.ts:325). The unit cost per hit (1) is faithful. (Whether MULTIPLE hits in one frame should stack, and the post-hit invulnerability window, are a separate divergence — see S-016.)

ours.verbatim: return { lives: Math.max(0, lives - 1), shieldHitAt: t }

title: 'Use the Force' bonus should scale with wave (5k/10k/25k/50k/100k), ours is a flat 5,000
claim: The clean-run Force bonus in the ROM is a per-wave table TSCFRC indexed by GM.WAV: wave 0=5,000; 1=10,000; 2=25,000; 3=50,000; 4+=100,000. Ours FORCE_BONUS = 5000 (flat), matching only wave 0. Waves 1+ are 2x-20x too low.
reasoning: TSCFRC rows (WSGAS:509-513, BCD): 00,50,00=5,000; 01,00,00=10,000; 02,50,00=25,000; 05,00,00=50,000; 10,00,00=100,000 ('LEVEL 5 AND ABOVE'). GETFRP (WSGAS:404-414) indexes GM.WAV*3 into TSCFRC, clamping at TSCFRZ-3 for GM.WAV>=5 (CMPB #TSCFRZ-TSCFRC/3 = #5). SCRFRC (WSGAS:440) is called from WSBASE:1169 ('JSR SCRFRC ;SCORE ONE FOR ASSERTIVENESS TRAINING(FORCEFUL)'), once per trench when Q.FRC marks the player 'used the Force'. This is a wave-indexed table; our single constant cannot express it. Fix is a per-wave lookup (e.g. FORCE_BONUS_BY_WAVE = [5000,10000,25000,50000,100000] clamped), not a constant tweak.

source.verbatim: TSCFRC:	.BYTE 00,50,00		;USE THE FORCE IN THE TRENCH

refutation_corrections: Minor (non-blocking): the proposed FORCE_BONUS_BY_WAVE is at GM.WAV (0-based) granularity, so a faithful port indexes it by (wave-1) since the clone's `wave` is 1-based; clamp any index >=4 to the 100,000 row.

title: Shield-energy-remaining wave bonus (5,000 per surviving shield unit) is absent
claim: At each wave's end the ROM awards 5,000 points per remaining shield unit (SCRSHL, table TSCSHL = .BYTE 00,50,00 -> 5,000, looped S.GAS times). Ours never converts surviving shields to score.
reasoning: SCRSHLD:: (WSGAS:375-401; 6-char symbol SCRSHL) loops B = S.GAS times, BCD-adding TSCSHL (WSGAS:525, 00,50,00 = 005000 = 5,000) each pass, then ADUSCRs the sum. It is called from WSMAIN:1967 ('JSR SCRSHL ;ADD SHIELD ENERGY BONUS') in the end-of-wave ceremony. So finishing a wave with 6 shields banks 30,000. Ours has no end-of-wave shield->score conversion anywhere (grep of gameRules/sim shows only object, port, all-towers and force bonuses). Straightforward to add: bonus = 5000 * remaining_shields on wave clear.

source.verbatim: TSCSHL:	.BYTE 00,50,00		;SHIELD SCORE FOR REMAINING ENERGY UNITS

title: Death-Star-selection wave bonus (200,000 x Death Star number) is absent
claim: The ROM grants a between-wave bonus for the Death Star the player selected: DS1=200,000, DS2=400,000, DS3=600,000, DS4=800,000 (TSCBN1-4, indexed by GM.WAV). Ours has no Death-Star selection and awards no such bonus.
reasoning: SCRWAV (WSGAS:327-340) computes GM.WAV*3 and indexes TSCBN1-3, adding TSCBN{n} (WSGAS:527-530: 20,00,00=200,000 / 40,00,00=400,000 / 60,00,00=600,000 / 80,00,00=800,000); 'WAVE 0 GETS NO BONUS' and it is gated on SC.FWV (first pass). Called from WSMAIN:1982 ('JSR SCRWAV ;AND ALSO ADD WAV BONUS'). This bonus rewards the player's choice of Death Star difficulty at the wave-select screen; the browser clone has linear waves and no difficulty-selection mechanic, so there is no site to award it.

title: Score-threshold extra shields (400,000 / 800,000) have no primary-source basis
claim: Ours awards +1 shield/life the first time the score crosses 400,000 and 800,000 (EXTRA_LIFE_THRESHOLDS, applied in finalizeScore). The primary source has NO score-threshold extra-shield mechanism: the strings 40000/80000 appear NOWHERE in the shipped source, and the ONLY shield-replenish path is ADCGAS — an operator-option amount (0-3 units, OPTS1) added only after the player KILLS the Death Star.
reasoning: The code comment cites the secondary doc's 'ROM extra-life text a40000/a80000'; grep of all *.MAC for 40000/80000/a40000/a80000 returns nothing, so that text does not exist in the LF primary (BOOK_WAS_WRONG). Every write to S.GAS in the shipped modules is: init (WSMAIN:1297), the per-hit DEC (WSGAS:76), and ADCGAS (WSGAS:42-58). ADCGAS is called ONLY from WSMAIN:1975 ('JSR ADCGAS ;ADD CANNED GAS'), immediately after 'LDA PT.LIV ;DID PLAYER KILL DETH STAR? / IFNE ;YES, SO ADD BONUS ENERGY' (WSMAIN:1973-1974) — i.e. shields are refilled on DEATH-STAR-KILL, not on a score threshold, and the amount is an operator option (WSGAS:44 'ANDA #03 ;HOW MUCH BONUS ENERGY?'), capped at the starting 6-9 (WSGAS:48-53). Ours also has no shield cap, so score-threshold awards can push lives past the ROM max of 9. Faithful fix: drop the invented score-threshold extra life and refill shields via an ADCGAS-style bonus on Death-Star kill, clamped to the 6-9 max.

source.verbatim: LDA PT.LIV		;DID PLAYER KILL DETH STAR?

refutation_corrections: The finding's S.GAS-write enumeration omits WSGAS:79 (STA S.GAS 'SET TO DEAD', the underflow pin to -1) — it belongs to the per-hit DEC/death path, not a replenish, so it does not weaken the thesis. The ADCGAS clamp is specifically to STARTING SHIELDS (6-9, operator-set), matching SWOPTS.DOC 'will not go higher than STARTING SHIELDS level' — worth carrying into the fix so the cap is the option value, not a hard-coded 9.

title: No post-hit shield-loss window: ours stacks simultaneous hits, ROM loses at most one shield per glow cycle
claim: The ROM loses at most ONE shield per hit event: a hit arms a ~10-game-frame glow (BG1GLW: 'LDA #10'), and DO1GAS refuses another decrement until the previous glow/animation finishes ('?AND DONE WITH PREVIOUS GAS CHANGE?'). Ours has no such gate — every colliding TIE and fireball in the same frame each does damage++, so a cluster can strip multiple shields at once and consecutive frames can drain with no invulnerability.
reasoning: BG1GLW (WSGLOW:58-64) only re-arms when GS.GLW==0 and sets S.GLW=10. DO1GAS (WSGAS:63-104) enters the decrement block only when GS.GLW>0 AND 'LDA GS.HIT / IFLE ;?AND DONE WITH PREVIOUS GAS CHANGE?' (WSGAS:67-68); it then sets GS.HIT=1 (busy) and does a single DEC S.GAS, and GS.HIT is not cleared until the multi-frame hit animation completes (GSVNEW, WSGAS:258-260). Net: one shield per hit, then a ~10-frame (~0.49s at 20.508 Hz) window before the next can register. Ours accumulates damage over both the enemy-collision and fireball-collision filters (sim.ts:307-323) with no cooldown/i-frame state, so multiple shields can be lost in a single 60 Hz frame. Fix needs a hit-cooldown flag on GameState, not a constant.

source.verbatim: IFLE			;?AND DONE WITH PREVIOUS GAS CHANGE?

refutation_corrections: Precision note (does not change verdict): the re-hit lockout is governed by GS.GLW/GS.HIT being cleared at the completion of the gauge-redraw animation (GSVNEW), not directly by the S.GLW=10 glow-display counter the finding cites; the ~10-game-frame / ~0.49s-at-20.508Hz figure is a good order-of-magnitude estimate of that window, not an exact ROM-pinned duration.
